steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the passage. The passage opens by declaring 'We are creatures of the sun. We love light and life' and immediately gives this as the reason people crowd into cities. It then develops the contrast between daytime comfort in nature and nighttime longing for lit streets and human company. The correct choice must capture why this human trait drives people toward urban life.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — To address common traits that motivate many people to choose to live in urban environments: matches exactly — the passage presents love of light and fear of darkness as the shared human traits behind urban migration. ✓
  • Choice B — To indicate that people sometimes experience feelings of isolation in crowded areas: reverses the passage's point; people seek crowded areas to escape loneliness, not because crowded areas cause it. ✗
  • Choice C — To illustrate the idea that most people tend to prefer hillsides in the country to certain aspects of towns and cities: the passage says people enjoy open hillsides in daytime but flee to cities at night — the overall preference runs the other way. ✗
  • Choice D — To caution people not to be so quick to dismiss the natural beauty that can be found in rural areas: the passage makes no such warning; it describes behavior, not advice. ✗
A is correct.
